rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Matrix contains at least one source link from each of the three standards for FOM-backed exchange scenarios.
rows.requirement_summary: Spec2025 requirements should cross-link framework/rules, federate interface, and OMT requirements.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Every service row has exactly one group and group-level coverage rollups are present.
rows.requirement_summary: Capability reports should be organized by federation management, declaration, object, ownership, time, DDM, and support services.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Callback inventory exists and each callback is supported, explicitly unsupported, or blocked with a reason.
rows.requirement_summary: RTI-initiated services must be tracked separately from federate-initiated services.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Route returns NotConnected or a structured equivalent; no raw crash.
rows.requirement_summary: A federate must connect to the RTI before creating/joining or otherwise interacting with federation execution.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Final backend/session state is disconnected and no live federate handles remain.
rows.implementation_target: All RTI backends and standard routes
rows.requirement_summary: A federate that resigns and has no further intent to participate should disconnect from the RTI.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: EVOKED delivers through evoke calls; IMMEDIATE delivers without explicit polling or reports unsupported until implemented.
rows.implementation_target: Java/Py4J/JPype adapters; C++ capsule callback contract
rows.requirement_summary: RTIs must implement both IMMEDIATE and EVOKED callback models.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each session has a distinct joined federate designator and callbacks route to the correct federate.
rows.implementation_target: InMemoryRTIEngine; Java shared kernel; C++ capsule session manager
rows.requirement_summary: An RTI must allow one application to participate as multiple joined federates and in multiple federations.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each illegal invocation returns the specified RTI exception type or structured equivalent.
rows.requirement_summary: Service invocations not allowed by the state charts must generate exceptions.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Both federates use the same time factory and grants are type-consistent.
rows.requirement_summary: RTI and joined federates in a federation execution must share a common logical time implementation.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: HLAinteger64Time and HLAfloat64Time or mapped equivalents are available and usable.
rows.requirement_summary: RTIs must provide two default logical time implementations: one integer and one floating point.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: No-FOM call fails; valid call succeeds and records HLAfloat64Time default.
rows.requirement_summary: Create Federation Execution requires at least one FOM module; if logical time is omitted, HLAfloat64Time is used.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: RTI returns a unique joined federate designator/name and current FOM is extended.
rows.requirement_summary: Join assigns or enforces a unique federate name and may add non-conflicting FOM modules.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Join fails with the expected FOM exception and current FOM remains unchanged.
rows.requirement_summary: Join Federation Execution must report errors for invalid, inconsistent, unreadable, or missing FOM modules.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: No artifact/route is labelled HLA Conforming unless certified external evidence is recorded.
rows.requirement_summary: The HLA Conforming label is reserved for publicly certified results from a recognized authority.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Report counts implemented and unsupported services; full conformance remains false until all are covered.
rows.requirement_summary: A conforming RTI must implement all joined-federate services and invoke all RTI-initiated services.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each report names the API/language verified and does not combine them into a single unqualified claim.
rows.implementation_target: Java/C++ standard shims; Python mapping docs
rows.requirement_summary: RTI conformance requires at least one implemented language-specific API, and results must name verified APIs.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Official Java/C++ compile reports exist; Python mapping notes link to requirements.
rows.requirement_summary: C++ and Java bindings are standard-defined; Python requires an explicit mapping.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Build fails if the artifact uses a hand-written mini API rather than official package/classes.
rows.requirement_summary: The Java API is defined by the listed files in the official Java API zip.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Both bridges return type-consistent Python logical time/interval values.
rows.requirement_summary: The Java API includes HLAfloat64 and HLAinteger64 time factories and logical time interfaces.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Demo with valid FOM records FOM evidence; demo without FOM warns or fails according to strict mode.
rows.requirement_summary: A federation must have an HLA FOM documented using the OMT.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: RTI evidence shows handle/class/routing metadata only; federate owns semantic payload state.
rows.implementation_target: hla.backends.* object registry; scenario federate state
rows.requirement_summary: Simulation-associated object instance representation belongs in federates rather than the RTI.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Normalized trace includes routeSelected, update/send invocation, and receiving callbacks; no direct federate shortcut.
rows.requirement_summary: FOM data exchanged among joined federates must flow through the RTI.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: All routes accept HLA-shaped calls and emit HLA-shaped callbacks or structured unsupported-service errors.
rows.requirement_summary: Joined federates must interact with the RTI using the HLA interface specification.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Only one owner is recorded; conflicting acquisition returns a structured ownership exception or unavailable callback.
rows.implementation_target: hla.backends.python1516e ownership; Java/C++ core ownership later
rows.requirement_summary: An instance attribute may be owned by at most one joined federate at a time.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Declared capabilities map to working publish/subscribe, callbacks, and trace events.
rows.implementation_target: hla.verification scenario suite; Java/C++ standard shims
rows.requirement_summary: Federates must be able to update/reflect attributes and send/receive interactions as specified in their SOMs.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Ownership callbacks and final owner state match expected transfer path; unsupported routes report explicit gaps.
rows.implementation_target: hla.backends.python1516e ownership; Java/C++ route parity later
rows.requirement_summary: Federates must be able to transfer and/or accept instance-attribute ownership dynamically as specified in SOMs.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Only changes satisfying the declared condition are sent; report marks capability unsupported until implemented.
rows.implementation_target: FOM/SOM validator; future update policy layer
rows.requirement_summary: Federates must be able to vary update conditions, such as thresholds, as specified in SOMs.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: timeRegulationEnabled, timeConstrainedEnabled, and timeAdvanceGrant appear in trace with expected logical time.
rows.requirement_summary: Federates must manage local time in a way that coordinates data exchange with other federation members.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: MOM area appears in capability matrix with explicit status rather than being omitted.
rows.requirement_summary: The implementation shall extract and plan support for the MOM area: Track MOM object class requirements as a management capability area.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: MOM area appears in capability matrix with explicit status rather than being omitted.
rows.requirement_summary: The implementation shall extract and plan support for the MOM area: Track MOM interaction class requirements as a management capability area.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: MOM area appears in capability matrix with explicit status rather than being omitted.
rows.requirement_summary: The implementation shall extract and plan support for the MOM area: Track RTI characteristics surfaced through MOM.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: MOM area appears in capability matrix with explicit status rather than being omitted.
rows.requirement_summary: The implementation shall extract and plan support for the MOM area: Track MOM service reporting requirements and reporting switches.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: MOM area appears in capability matrix with explicit status rather than being omitted.
rows.requirement_summary: The implementation shall extract and plan support for the MOM area: Track MOM OMT tables as object-model fixtures.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Standard MIM is inserted or reported as auto-provided; final FDD has required MOM data.
rows.requirement_summary: All FOMs require MOM inclusion through a MIM; SOMs include MIM depending on MOM capabilities.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Reports use evidence-backed terms unless external certification evidence is recorded.
rows.requirement_summary: Object models and tools may use HLA Conforming only when certified results are publicly available.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Conforming XML passes; invalid user input is explicitly identified before output.
rows.requirement_summary: A conforming object model tool accepts conforming models, parses XML, and identifies user inputs that would produce nonconforming models.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Usage table entries are accepted/rejected or generated according to the requirement and linked to route trace evidence.
rows.requirement_summary: Service usage documentation shall/may be supported: Service utilization table shall include clause number, service name, and usage columns.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Usage table entries are accepted/rejected or generated according to the requirement and linked to route trace evidence.
rows.requirement_summary: Service usage documentation shall/may be supported: FOM service usage table shall mark the minimum federation-forming services as Yes.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Usage table entries are accepted/rejected or generated according to the requirement and linked to route trace evidence.
rows.requirement_summary: Service usage documentation shall/may be supported: SOM service usage table shall mark the minimum federate-forming services as Yes.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Usage table entries are accepted/rejected or generated according to the requirement and linked to route trace evidence.
rows.requirement_summary: Service usage documentation shall/may be supported: Federations and federates may document HLA service usage in FOM/SOM modules.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each route trace includes FR/FI/OMT requirement IDs for the exercised capabilities.
rows.requirement_summary: Every route evidence trace should include the requirement IDs exercised by the scenario.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Lifecycle-only routes are not labelled core-green or full-interface-complete.
rows.requirement_summary: Reports must distinguish lifecycle/core-exchange evidence from full joined-service and RTI-initiated callback coverage.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each row names the language/API and has independent status and unsupported-service ledger.
rows.implementation_target: standard_shims; Java/C++ intake; Python mapping docs
rows.requirement_summary: Java, C++, and Python mapping evidence must be tracked separately.

rows.expected_result_from_extraction: Each route returns equivalent structured error metadata without process crash.
rows.requirement_summary: Illegal operations and invalid FOMs should produce structured HLA exceptions or validation errors across Python, Java, and C++ routes.

scope: 2025 executable packet traceability rows mapped to direct evidence anchors, or to explicit umbrella child evidence / packet-only no-direct-anchor states
